prompt: I want to build an MCP server that lets Claude query our internal Snowflake warehouse and rewrites SQL on the fly when joins get expensive (using an LLM to inspect EXPLAIN plans and propose a cheaper rewrite). Spike this before I commit to building the production server. Don't actually run anything against Snowflake or Anthropic — assume no credentials in this environment — but produce the artifacts you would create on day 0.

assertions.description: An assumptions list file is present in outputs (any markdown file whose name or first heading mentions 'assumption').
Assumptions are categorized as must-hold / should-hold / nice-if-it-holds (or close synonyms like 'must / should / nice').
There are between 2 and 4 must-hold assumptions (the skill's 'sweet spot').
Each must-hold assumption has a measurable, falsifiable go/no-go gate (e.g., HTTP code, numeric threshold, pass/fail count). 'It works' alone does not qualify.
A single Python (or single-file) spike exists in outputs whose filename contains 'spike'. No package structure beneath outputs/.
The spike imports the real SDKs it would use in production (e.g., snowflake-connector-python and anthropic), not mocks.
The spike file has no Pydantic models, no FastAPI/MCP routes wired up, no separate config files alongside it. It is one file with hardcoded inputs.
`tradeoffs.md` has rows or sections for each must-hold assumption containing the assumption / gate / result / learning / pivot structure (all five elements present).
An explicit decision is stated, matching one of: build / pivot architecture / pivot narrative / stop.

At least one must-hold assumption is about LLM behavior (e.g., 'Claude can read EXPLAIN plans and propose a cheaper rewrite that the warehouse actually accepts and that runs faster'), with a measurable gate.
